refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the latency of data acquisition and processing separately and combined, ensuring the p95 latency is ≤ 1.0 s under supported update scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry data streams for data acquisition
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency

coupling_check.notes: DP1 and DP2 are logically decoupled; data acquisition and processing can be modularized with clear interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports required telemetry bandwidth and latency
Risk of processing delays due to unexpected data volume spikes
Safety criticality requires robust error handling in both acquisition and processing
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry network performance and processing load; consider fallback modes if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry acquisition and real-time processing to update the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from vehicles and sensors

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between vehicles, teleoperation, and safety services

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as NGHE Solution integrates both acquisition and processing; low diffusion as functionality is centralized.
Vehicle module primarily provides raw data; limited containment of processing logic.
Teleoperation focuses on control and tasking, not on worksite model updates.
Safety Services handle alerts and policies, not data acquisition or processing.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update latency functionality within the NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability and verification.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes too complex
Integration risks if interfaces with Vehicle telemetry or Network Link are unstable
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure modular internal architecture within NGHE Solution to manage complexity; define robust interface contracts.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are clearly decomposed and mapped to logical DPs addressing acquisition and processing latency, with low coupling.

diffusion_risks: Centralizing both acquisition and processing in NGHE Solution may increase module complexity
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the best ownership candidate with minimal diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end and at sub-function levels using telemetry and processing logs.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity within NGHE Solution to avoid integration bottlenecks
Validate telemetry network performance assumptions early

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with clear ownership and manageable risks. Verification approaches are feasible and interfaces are well-defined.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports required telemetry bandwidth and latency
Processing algorithms can meet real-time constraints
open_risks: Potential processing delays under high data volume
